title: RUNWAY / FW
subCategory: 111 RUNWAYS Series 111 Category Codes include criteria for runways for fixed wing aircraft and runways or landing pads for rotary wing aircraft. Runways are prepared surfaces for the landing and takeoff of both fixed wing and rotary wing aircraft. Landing pads are prepared surfaces for the Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) of rotary wing aircraft (including V-22).
notes: PREPARED SURFACES FOR THE LANDING AND TAKEOFF OF AIRCRAFT.
description: PREPARED SURFACES FOR THE LANDING AND TAKEOFF OF AIRCRAFT.

title: RUNWAY OVERRUN - PAVED
subCategory: 111 RUNWAYS Series 111 Category Codes include criteria for runways for fixed wing aircraft and runways or landing pads for rotary wing aircraft. Landing pads are prepared surfaces for the Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) of rotary wing aircraft (including V-22).
notes: AREAS EXTENDING AT EACH END OF A RUNWAY. THE RUNWAY OVERRUN AREAS ARE REQUIRED TO REDUCE SERIOUS DAMAGE TO AN AIRCRAFT IN THE EVENT THAT THE AIRCRAFT RUNS OFF OF THE RUNWAY END DURING TAKEOFF OR LANDING.
description: AREAS EXTENDING AT EACH END OF A RUNWAY. THE RUNWAY OVERRUN AREAS ARE REQUIRED TO REDUCE SERIOUS DAMAGE TO AN AIRCRAFT IN THE EVENT THAT THE AIRCRAFT RUNS OFF OF THE RUNWAY END DURING TAKEOFF OR LANDING.

subCategory: 112 TAXIWAYS

title: ACFT DIRECT FUELING STA
subCategory: 121 AIRCRAFT FUELNG/DISPNG Refueler trucks are the preferred method to fuel aircraft. However, direct fueling stations may be considered for: (1) carrier aircraft, including helicopters, when the mission dictates a continuing need for rapid turnaround without shutting engines down, (2) cargo/transport aircraft with prescribed short ground times or (3) patrol aircraft which require an average refueling of 2500 gallons or more.
notes: AIRCRAFT DIRECT FUELING STATIONS PROVIDE OUTLETS WHERE AIRCRAFT CAN BE FUELED FROM A CLOSED CIRCUIT FUEL SYSTEM AS OPPOSED TO REFUELER TRUCKS.
description: AIRCRAFT DIRECT FUELING STATIONS PROVIDE OUTLETS WHERE AIRCRAFT CAN BE FUELED FROM A CLOSED CIRCUIT FUEL SYSTEM AS OPPOSED TO REFUELER TRUCKS.

title: ACFT TRUCK FUELING FAC
subCategory: 121 AIRCRAFT FUELNG/DISPNG Refueler trucks are the preferred method to fuel aircraft. However, direct fueling stations may be considered for: (1) carrier aircraft, including helicopters, when the mission dictates a continuing need for rapid turnaround without shutting engines down, (2) cargo/transport aircraft with prescribed short ground times or (3) patrol aircraft which require an average refueling of 2500 gallons or more.
notes: AN AIRCRAFT TRUCK FUELING FACILITY IS USED TO TRANSFER FUEL TO AIRCRAFT REFUELING TRUCKS.
description: AN AIRCRAFT TRUCK FUELING FACILITY IS USED TO TRANSFER FUEL TO AIRCRAFT REFUELING TRUCKS.

title: AIRCRAFT DEFUELNG FACILITY
subCategory: 121 AIRCRAFT FUELNG/DISPNG Refueler trucks are the preferred method to fuel aircraft. notes: THIS CATEGORY CODE IS FOR DEFUELING AIRCRAFT OF EXCESS OR RESIDUAL FUEL. AIRCRAFT SHALL BE DEFUELED INTO TANK TRUCKS DESIGNATED FOR THAT PURPOSE.
description: THIS CATEGORY CODE IS FOR DEFUELING AIRCRAFT OF EXCESS OR RESIDUAL FUEL. AIRCRAFT SHALL BE DEFUELED INTO TANK TRUCKS DESIGNATED FOR THAT PURPOSE.

title: MARINE FUELING FACILITY
subCategory: 122 MARINE FUEL DISPENSING This basic Category Code includes the following: Marine fueling facilities designed for small vessels and capital ships that should be able to refuel the largest ship that can dock at the station¿s waterfront. Small craft fueling stations used to refuel such small craft as crash boats and administrative boats. Marine ready fuel storage tanks for the ready issue operation storage of a particular grade of fuel for small boats and yard craft.
notes: A MARINE FUELING FACILITY IS DESIGNED FOR SMALL VESSELS AND CAPITAL SHIPS AND SHOULD BE ABLE TO REFUEL THE LARGEST SHIP THAT CAN DOCK AT THE STATION'S WATERFRONT.
description: A MARINE FUELING FACILITY IS DESIGNED FOR SMALL VESSELS AND CAPITAL SHIPS AND SHOULD BE ABLE TO REFUEL THE LARGEST SHIP THAT CAN DOCK AT THE STATION'S WATERFRONT.

title: SMALL CRAFT FUELING STA
subCategory: 122 MARINE FUEL DISPENSING This basic Category Code includes the following: Marine fueling facilities designed for small vessels and capital ships that should be able to refuel the largest ship that can dock at the station¿s waterfront. Small craft fueling stations used to refuel such small craft as crash boats and administrative boats. Marine ready fuel storage tanks for the ready issue operation storage of a particular grade of fuel for small boats and yard craft.
notes: A SMALL CRAFT FUELING STATION IS USED TO REFUEL SUCH SMALL CRAFT AS CRASH BOATS AND ADMINISTRATIVE BOATS. IT SHALL INCLUDE DISPENSING PEDESTAL-TYPE COMMERCIAL PUMPS, PIPING, TANKS, HOSES, FLOODLIGHTS AND GROUNDING DEVICES, ELECTRICAL POWER, AND FIRE PROTECTION.
description: A SMALL CRAFT FUELING STATION IS USED TO REFUEL SUCH SMALL CRAFT AS CRASH BOATS AND ADMINISTRATIVE BOATS. IT SHALL INCLUDE DISPENSING PEDESTAL-TYPE COMMERCIAL PUMPS, PIPING, TANKS, HOSES, FLOODLIGHTS AND GROUNDING DEVICES, ELECTRICAL POWER, AND FIRE PROTECTION.

title: SM CRAFT READY FUEL STRG

Small craft fueling stations used to refuel such small craft as crash boats and administrative boats. Marine ready fuel storage tanks for the ready issue operation storage of a particular grade of fuel for small boats and yard craft.
notes: A MARINE READY FUEL STORAGE TANK IS THE READY ISSUE OPERATIONAL STORAGE OF A PARTICULAR GRADE OF FUEL FOR SMALL BOATS. (E.G. SMALL TUGS, SECURITY BOATS, REPAIR BARGES AND YARD CRAFT)
description: A MARINE READY FUEL STORAGE TANK IS THE READY ISSUE OPERATIONAL STORAGE OF A PARTICULAR GRADE OF FUEL FOR SMALL BOATS. (E.G. SMALL TUGS, SECURITY BOATS, REPAIR BARGES AND YARD CRAFT)

title: FILLING STATION
subCategory: 123 LAND VEH FUELNG/DISPNG This Category Code group is for facilities serving official government land vehicles and equipment only. If NEX operates the facility, see Category Code 740-30/31, Exchange Service and Auto Repair/Supplemental Gasoline Station. For Aviation Fueling and Dispensing, see Code series 122. For bulk fuel storage such as tank farm installation, see Category Code series 411.
notes: A FILLING STATION IS A FUELING FACILITY FOR OFFICIAL VEHICLES AND EQUIPMENT ON NAVY AND MARINE CORPS INSTALLATIONS. THIS CATEGORY CODE APPLIES TO PUMP OUTLETS INCLUDING THE COVERED ISLANDS THAT SUPPORT THE PUMP OUTLETS, THE CONCRETE PARKING AREA, LIGHTING AND THE ACCESS PAVING TO THE PUMPS/ISLANDS.
description: A FILLING STATION IS A FUELING FACILITY FOR OFFICIAL VEHICLES AND EQUIPMENT ON NAVY AND MARINE CORPS INSTALLATIONS. THIS CATEGORY CODE APPLIES TO PUMP OUTLETS INCLUDING THE COVERED ISLANDS THAT SUPPORT THE PUMP OUTLETS, THE CONCRETE PARKING AREA, LIGHTING AND THE ACCESS PAVING TO THE PUMPS/ISLANDS.

title: FILLING STATION BUILDING
subCategory: 123 LAND VEH FUELNG/DISPNG This Category Code group is for facilities serving official government land vehicles and equipment only. If NEX operates the facility, see Category Code 740-30/31, Exchange Service and Auto Repair/Supplemental Gasoline Station. For Aviation Fueling and Dispensing, see Code series 122. For bulk fuel storage such as tank farm installation, see Category Code series 411.
notes: THIS CODE IS USED FOR REPORTING THE ADMINISTRATIVE SHELTER ASSOCIATED WITH A FILLING STATION. IF THE FILLING STATION IS OPERATED BY A PRIVATE ENTITY, THEN USE THE 740-30/31 CATEGORY CODES. WHERE CREDIT CARD SYSTEMS ARE USED AND OPERATORS ARE NOT NEEDED, A SHELTER IS NOT REQUIRED.
description: THIS CODE IS USED FOR REPORTING THE ADMINISTRATIVE SHELTER ASSOCIATED WITH A FILLING STATION. IF THE FILLING STATION IS OPERATED BY A PRIVATE ENTITY, THEN USE THE 740-30/31 CATEGORY CODES. WHERE CREDIT CARD SYSTEMS ARE USED AND OPERATORS ARE NOT NEEDED, A SHELTER IS NOT REQUIRED.

description: TRANSFER PIPELINES USED FOR THE TRANSFER AND TRANSPORT OF PETROLEUM, OILS, LUBRICANTS AND FUELS BETWEEN SITES.

title: POL PIPELINE PUMP STA
subCategory: 125 POL PIPELINE This category is for pipelines and accessory equipment between tank farms and operating fuel storage facilities and intermediate points.
notes: PUMPING STATIONS AND ANCILLARY EQUIPMENT USED TO MOVE THE FUEL THROUGH THE PIPES. THIS FACILITY MAY ALSO INCLUDE CONTROLS, GAUGES, METER, LIGHTING, FIRE PROTECTION, AND VENTILATION.

title: SHLTR FOR PUMP STA

notes: THIS IS USED FOR STRUCTURES HOUSING PUMPING STATIONS AND ANCILLARY EQUIPMENT.
description: THIS IS USED FOR STRUCTURES HOUSING PUMPING STATIONS AND ANCILLARY EQUIPMENT.

title: POL PIPING- SINGLE SITE
subCategory: 125 POL PIPELINE This category is for pipelines and accessory equipment between tank farms and operating fuel storage facilities and intermediate points.
notes: POL PIPELINES USED FOR THE TRANSFER AND TRANSPORT OF PETROLEUM, OILS, LUBRICANTS AND FUELS WITHIN A SITE (EXCEPT THOSE WITHIN A MARINE OR AIRCRAFT FUELING FACILITY).
description: POL PIPELINES USED FOR THE TRANSFER AND TRANSPORT OF PETROLEUM, OILS, LUBRICANTS AND FUELS WITHIN A SITE (EXCEPT THOSE WITHIN A MARINE OR AIRCRAFT FUELING FACILITY).

title: SURGE STORAGE
subCategory: 125 POL PIPELINE This category is for pipelines and accessory equipment between tank farms and operating fuel storage facilities and intermediate points.
notes: A SURGE TANK IS USED WHERE THERE IS A RISK OF HYDRAULIC SHOCK. HYDRAULIC SHOCK CAN OCCUR WHEN THE PUMP USED TO DELIVER FUEL IS GREATER THAN THE PIPELINE CAPACITY; UNLOADING RATE OF THE DELIVERY TANKER/BARGE EXCEEDS THE RATE OF THE SHORE PUMPING SYSTEM; OR WATER, AIR OR OTHER BLOCKAGE OCCURS IN THE PIPELINE.
description: A SURGE TANK IS USED WHERE THERE IS A RISK OF HYDRAULIC SHOCK. HYDRAULIC SHOCK CAN OCCUR WHEN THE PUMP USED TO DELIVER FUEL IS GREATER THAN THE PIPELINE CAPACITY; UNLOADING RATE OF THE DELIVERY TANKER/BARGE EXCEEDS THE RATE OF THE SHORE PUMPING SYSTEM; OR WATER, AIR OR OTHER BLOCKAGE OCCURS IN THE PIPELINE.

title: DRUM AND CAN LDING FACY
subCategory: 126 OTHER LIQUID FUELNG/DISPNG Use this Category Code for liquid fuel or petroleum products facilities not specifically related to aviation, marine craft, or ground vehicle fuel requirements (see Category Codes series 121, 122, and 123, respectively). Use Category Code 821 60 or 821 61 for heating plant fuel storage. This Category Code includes fuel loading and unloading; drum storage, loading and maintenance; and miscellaneous fuel storage (i.e., heating fuel, kerosene, propane, fuel oil, etc.). See Category Code series 411 for depot level storage.

description: A DRUM AND CAN LOADING FACILITY IS A FUEL FACILITY EQUIPPED TO FILL DRUMS WITH FUEL OIL, DIESEL, KEROSENE, JET ENGINE FUEL, MOTOR GASOLINE, AVIATION GASOLINE, AND LUBRICATING OILS. THE FACILITY MAY ALSO BE PROVIDED WITH A DRUM RECONDITIONING PLANT.

title: CABLE HOUSE
subCategory: 131 COMMUNICATIONS - BUILDINGS This group of Shore Facilities supports the reception, processing, distribution, and/or transmission of classified and unclassified voice, data, and video communications in support of the Department of Navy operational missions.
notes: A CABLE HOUSE IS AN UNMANNED FACILITY THAT FUNCTIONS EITHER AS AN EXTERNAL JUNCTION POINT FOR COAXIAL CABLES, OR AS MECHANICAL SPACE FOR SUPPORT EQUIPMENT ASSOCIATED WITH EXTREMELY LOW FREQUENCY (ELF) AND VERY LOW FREQUENCY (VLF) ANTENNAS.

title: COMM, INFO, OR INTEL FAC
subCategory: 131 COMMUNICATIONS - BUILDINGS This group of Shore Facilities supports the reception, processing, distribution, and/or transmission of classified and unclassified voice, data, and video communications in support of the Department of Navy operational missions.
notes: COMMUNICATIONS, INFORMATION, OR INTELLIGENCE FACILITIES ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR INFORMATION PROCESSING, DELIVERY OF INFORMATION SERVICES, AND INFORMATION/DATA STORAGE. THESE ULTIMATELY SUPPORT THE JOINT INFORMATION ENVIRONMENT (JIE). THE JIE IDENTIFIES SEVERAL DIFFERENT TYPES OF FUNCTIONAL NODES: COMPUTING NODES (E.G. DATA CENTERS), COMMUNICATION NODES (E.G. NETWORK GATEWAYS), AND OPERATIONS NODES (E.G. ENTERPRISE OPERATION CENTERS). THESE NODES SHOULD BE VIEWED AS FUNCTIONAL ENCLAVES, NOT SEPARATE FACILITIES; IN FACT, MULTIPLE NODES AND NODE TYPES MAY BE PRESENT IN A SINGLE PHYSICAL FACILITY.

title: COMMS RELAY BLDG

title: SAT COMMS FAC

title: AUTO COMMS SWITCH CNTR

title: MIL AFFIL RADIO STA (MARS)

title: RAWIN BUILDING
subCategory: 133 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - BLDGS Basic Category Code group 133 applies to those Air Traffic Control Facilities (ATCFs) that contain the equipment, devices, and personnel responsible for air traffic control and navigational aids. This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: A RAWIN BUILDING (RADAR WIND SOUNDING) IS A SPECIALIZED WEATHER REPORTING FACILITY. IT HOUSES TRACKING EQUIPMENT USED IN CONJUNCTION WITH BALLOONBORNE RADIOSONDE TRANSMITTERS.
description: A RAWIN BUILDING (RADAR WIND SOUNDING) IS A SPECIALIZED WEATHER REPORTING FACILITY. IT HOUSES TRACKING EQUIPMENT USED IN CONJUNCTION WITH BALLOONBORNE RADIOSONDE TRANSMITTERS.

title: VHF OMNI-DIR RNG (VOR) FAC

Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: THE VERY HIGH FREQUENCY (VHF) OMNI-DIRECTIONAL RANGE (VOR) BUILDING HOUSES A VHF, FIXED GROUND-BASED STATION WHICH CONTINUOUSLY TRANSMITS BEARING, IDENTIFICATION, AND WITH PROPER EQUIPMENT, DISTANCE INFORMATION TO PROPERLY EQUIPPED AIRCRAFT.
description: THE VERY HIGH FREQUENCY (VHF) OMNI-DIRECTIONAL RANGE (VOR) BUILDING HOUSES A VHF, FIXED GROUND-BASED STATION WHICH CONTINUOUSLY TRANSMITS BEARING, IDENTIFICATION, AND WITH PROPER EQUIPMENT, DISTANCE INFORMATION TO PROPERLY EQUIPPED AIRCRAFT.

title: TACAN BUILDING

This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: THE TACTICAL AIR NAVIGATION (TACAN) BUILDING HOUSES UHF GROUND-BASED STATION WHICH TRANSMITS BEARING, IDENTIFICATION, AND DISTANCE INFORMATION TO PROPERLY EQUIPPED AIRCRAFT. THE TACAN IS PRIMARILY A MILITARY SHORT-RANGE 322 KM (200 MILE) NAVIGATIONAL AID THAT IS GENERALLY PLANNED FOR EACH NAVY AND MARINE CORPS AIR STATION.

This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.

description: THIS NON-DIRECTIONAL BEACON (NDB) FACILITY IS AN UNATTENDED FACILITY WHICH HOUSES ELECTRIC EQUIPMENT (RADIO BEACON) USED TO TRANSMIT A NON-DIRECTIONAL RADIO SIGNAL PATTERN TO AIRCRAFT EQUIPPED WITH AUTOMATIC RADIO DIRECTION AND FINDING (ADF) EQUIPMENT.

title: AIR NAVIGATION BUILDING
subCategory: 133 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - BLDGS Basic Category Code group 133 applies to those Air Traffic Control Facilities (ATCFs) that contain the equipment, devices, and personnel responsible for air traffic control and navigational aids. This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: AN AIR NAVIGATION BUILDING IS A SPECIALIZED FACILITY FOR PROVIDING A READILY AVAILABLE SOURCE OF OPERATIONAL AND AERONAUTICAL INTELLIGENCE INFORMATION; STORAGE AND ISSUE OF AERONAUTICAL MAPS AND CHARTS; AND SECURE STORAGE OF CLASSIFIED MATERIAL UP TO TOP SECRET DOCUMENTS.
description: AN AIR NAVIGATION BUILDING IS A SPECIALIZED FACILITY FOR PROVIDING A READILY AVAILABLE SOURCE OF OPERATIONAL AND AERONAUTICAL INTELLIGENCE INFORMATION; STORAGE AND ISSUE OF AERONAUTICAL MAPS AND CHARTS; AND SECURE STORAGE OF CLASSIFIED MATERIAL UP TO TOP SECRET DOCUMENTS.

title: RATCF FACILITY
subCategory: 133 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - BLDGS Basic Category Code group 133 applies to those Air Traffic Control Facilities (ATCFs) that contain the equipment, devices, and personnel responsible for air traffic control and navigational aids. This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: THE RADAR AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL FACILITY (RATCF) PROVIDES HOUSING FOR EQUIPMENT AND PERSONNEL TO SUPPORT INSTRUMENT FLIGHT RULES (IFR) CONTROL OF AIRCRAFT ON APPROACH TO OR DEPARTURE FROM THE TERMINAL RADAR FACILITY OR AIRPORT. OTHER FUNCTIONS INCLUDE PRECISION APPROACH RADAR (PAR) FOR LANDING AIRCRAFT DURING INCLEMENT WEATHER AND LIMITED VISIBILITY.
description: THE RADAR AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL FACILITY (RATCF) PROVIDES HOUSING FOR EQUIPMENT AND PERSONNEL TO SUPPORT INSTRUMENT FLIGHT RULES (IFR) CONTROL OF AIRCRAFT ON APPROACH TO OR DEPARTURE FROM THE TERMINAL RADAR FACILITY OR AIRPORT. OTHER FUNCTIONS INCLUDE PRECISION APPROACH RADAR (PAR) FOR LANDING AIRCRAFT DURING INCLEMENT WEATHER AND LIMITED VISIBILITY.

title: MTRACON
subCategory: 133 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - BLDGS Basic Category Code group 133 applies to those Air Traffic Control Facilities (ATCFs) that contain the equipment, devices, and personnel responsible for air traffic control and navigational aids. This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: A MILITARY TERMINAL RADAR APPROACH CONTROL (MTRACON) FACILITY IS USED TO CONTROL AIR TRAFFIC TO PROVIDE SAFE, EXPEDITIOUS, AND ORDERLY MOVEMENT OF AIRCRAFT UNDER ALL WEATHER CONDITIONS. JUSTIFICATION FOR A MTRACON IS ESTABLISHED BY THE CHIEF OF NAVAL OPERATIONS.
description: A MILITARY TERMINAL RADAR APPROACH CONTROL (MTRACON) FACILITY IS USED TO CONTROL AIR TRAFFIC TO PROVIDE SAFE, EXPEDITIOUS, AND ORDERLY MOVEMENT OF AIRCRAFT UNDER ALL WEATHER CONDITIONS. JUSTIFICATION FOR A MTRACON IS ESTABLISHED BY THE CHIEF OF NAVAL OPERATIONS.

title: FACSFAC
subCategory: 133 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - BLDGS Basic Category Code group 133 applies to those Air Traffic Control Facilities (ATCFs) that contain the equipment, devices, and personnel responsible for air traffic control and navigational aids. This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: THE FLEET AREA CONTROL SURVEILLANCE FACILITY (FACSFAC) BUILDING HOUSES THE FACSFAC TRACKING SYSTEM (FACTS) AND NAVY TACTICAL DATA SYSTEM/ADVANCED COMBAT DIRECTION SYSTEM (NTDS/ACDS) EQUIPMENT AND PERSONNEL TO PROVIDE A VARIETY OF SERVICES TO AIR, SURFACE AND SUBSURFACE UNITS.

title: JCF FACILITY
subCategory: 133 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - BLDGS Basic Category Code group 133 applies to those Air Traffic Control Facilities (ATCFs) that contain the equipment, devices, and personnel responsible for air traffic control and navigational aids. This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: THE JOINT CONTROL FACILITY (JCF) IS AN AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL FACILITY, A RADAR AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL FACILITY (RATCF), AND A RANGE OPERATIONS CENTER (ROC) UNDER ONE ROOF.
description: THE JOINT CONTROL FACILITY (JCF) IS AN AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL FACILITY, A RADAR AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL FACILITY (RATCF), AND A RANGE OPERATIONS CENTER (ROC) UNDER ONE ROOF.

title: AIR SURVEILLANCE RADAR FAC
subCategory: 133 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - BLDGS Basic Category Code group 133 applies to those Air Traffic Control Facilities (ATCFs) that contain the equipment, devices, and personnel responsible for air traffic control and navigational aids. This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: THE AIR SURVEILLANCE RADAR (ASR) FACILITY IS AN UNATTENDED FACILITY WHICH SERVES AS A MAJOR COMPONENT OF THE RADAR AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL FACILITY (RATCF); THE MILITARY TERMINAL APPROACH CONTROL FACILITY (MTRACON); AND THE JOINT CONTROL FACILITY (JCF). IT PROVIDES DETECTION AND IDENTIFICATION FOR CONTROL OF AIRCRAFT OPERATING IN A LINE-OF-SIGHT RANGE AND ALTITUDES DETERMINED BY SYSTEM DESIGN.

IT PROVIDES DETECTION AND IDENTIFICATION FOR CONTROL OF AIRCRAFT OPERATING IN A LINE-OF-SIGHT RANGE AND ALTITUDES DETERMINED BY SYSTEM DESIGN.

title: ARSR FACILITY
subCategory: 133 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - BLDGS Basic Category Code group 133 applies to those Air Traffic Control Facilities (ATCFs) that contain the equipment, devices, and personnel responsible for air traffic control and navigational aids. notes: THE AIR ROUTE SURVEILLANCE RADAR (ARSR) FACILITY HOUSES THE ELECTRONIC LONG RANGE RADAR SYSTEM USED TO OBTAIN THE RANGE AND AZIMUTH OF AN AIRCRAFT.
description: THE AIR ROUTE SURVEILLANCE RADAR (ARSR) FACILITY HOUSES THE ELECTRONIC LONG RANGE RADAR SYSTEM USED TO OBTAIN THE RANGE AND AZIMUTH OF AN AIRCRAFT.

title: WHEELS WATCH SHELTER
subCategory: 133 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - BLDGS Basic Category Code group 133 applies to those Air Traffic Control Facilities (ATCFs) that contain the equipment, devices, and personnel responsible for air traffic control and navigational aids. This group discusses complete air traffic control classes and systems, which are defined below. Other elements of air traffic control and navigation aids that are remotely located around the airfield can be found in the 133, 134 and 135 series of Category Codes.
notes: A PORTABLE WHEELS WATCH BOOTH IS PROVIDED WITH THE RUNWAY WHEELS- UP/WAVE-OFF LIGHTING SYSTEM, CATEGORY CODE 136 45. THE SHELTER IS LOCATED APPROXIMATELY 302 METERS (990 FEET) SHORT OF THE RUNWAY THRESHOLD NEAR THE WHEELS- UP/WAVE-OFF LIGHTING SYSTEM. THE FACILITY MAY BE EITHER A TRAILER OR TRUCK.
description: A PORTABLE WHEELS WATCH BOOTH IS PROVIDED WITH THE RUNWAY WHEELS- UP/WAVE-OFF LIGHTING SYSTEM, CATEGORY CODE 136 45. THE SHELTER IS LOCATED APPROXIMATELY 302 METERS (990 FEET) SHORT OF THE RUNWAY THRESHOLD NEAR THE WHEELS- UP/WAVE-OFF LIGHTING SYSTEM. THE FACILITY MAY BE EITHER A TRAILER OR TRUCK.

title: ANTENNA - NAVIGATION
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: AN ANTENNA SYSTEM FOR NAVIGATION AID WILL VARY WITH THE TYPE AND PURPOSE OF THE NAVIGATIONAL AID. THIS CATEGORY CODE SHALL BE USED TO INDICATE ENTIRE ANTENNA SYSTEMS.
description: AN ANTENNA SYSTEM FOR NAVIGATION AID WILL VARY WITH THE TYPE AND PURPOSE OF THE NAVIGATIONAL AID. THIS CATEGORY CODE SHALL BE USED TO INDICATE ENTIRE ANTENNA SYSTEMS.

title: AP/HP BEACON
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: AN AIRPORT AND/OR HELIPORT BEACON IS AN INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNIZED ROTATING OR FLASHING ILLUMINATED BEACON OPERATED AS A VISUAL AID TO AIR NAVIGATION TO ASSIST AIRCREWS IN LOCATING AND IDENTIFYING AIRPORTS AND/OR HELIPORTS.
description: AN AIRPORT AND/OR HELIPORT BEACON IS AN INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNIZED ROTATING OR FLASHING ILLUMINATED BEACON OPERATED AS A VISUAL AID TO AIR NAVIGATION TO ASSIST AIRCREWS IN LOCATING AND IDENTIFYING AIRPORTS AND/OR HELIPORTS.

title: GROUND CONTROL APPR SYST
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: A GROUND CONTROL APPROACH (GCA) SYSTEM IS A RADAR APPROACH SYSTEM OPERATED FROM THE GROUND BY AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL PERSONNEL TRANSMITTING INSTRUCTIONS TO THE PILOT BY RADIO.
description: A GROUND CONTROL APPROACH (GCA) SYSTEM IS A RADAR APPROACH SYSTEM OPERATED FROM THE GROUND BY AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL PERSONNEL TRANSMITTING INSTRUCTIONS TO THE PILOT BY RADIO.

title: PREC APPR RADAR
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: THE PRECISION APPROACH RADAR (PAR) IS AN UNATTENDED SELF-CONTAINED RADAR SYSTEM. THE PAR DETECTS AZIMUTH, ELEVATION, AND RANGE INFORMATION OF AIRCRAFT ON FINAL LANDING APPROACH TO PAR INSTRUMENTED RUNWAYS. THE INFORMATION IS DISPLAYED IN THE MILITARY TERMINAL RADAR APPROACH CONTROL (MTRACON) FACILITY.
description: THE PRECISION APPROACH RADAR (PAR) IS AN UNATTENDED SELF-CONTAINED RADAR SYSTEM. THE PAR DETECTS AZIMUTH, ELEVATION, AND RANGE INFORMATION OF AIRCRAFT ON FINAL LANDING APPROACH TO PAR INSTRUMENTED RUNWAYS. THE INFORMATION IS DISPLAYED IN THE MILITARY TERMINAL RADAR APPROACH CONTROL (MTRACON) FACILITY.

title: PREC APPR LAND SYS
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: THE PRECISION APPROACH LANDING SYSTEM (PALS) IS AN UNATTENDED, SELF-CONTAINED RADAR SYSTEM. THE PALS DETECTS AZIMUTH, ELEVATION, AND RANGE INFORMATION OF AIRCRAFT ON FINAL APPROACH TO PALS INSTRUMENTED RUNWAYS.
description: THE PRECISION APPROACH LANDING SYSTEM (PALS) IS AN UNATTENDED, SELF-CONTAINED RADAR SYSTEM. THE PALS DETECTS AZIMUTH, ELEVATION, AND RANGE INFORMATION OF AIRCRAFT ON FINAL APPROACH TO PALS INSTRUMENTED RUNWAYS.

title: INSTR LAND SYS
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: THE INSTRUMENT LANDING SYSTEM (ILS) PROVIDES AZIMUTH, DISTANCE, ELEVATION, AND GLIDE PATH POSITION TO AIRCRAFT ON A PRECISION APPROACH TO THE ILS INSTRUMENTED RUNWAY. THE ILS OPERATES IN THE VHF AND UHF RADIO BANDS.
description: THE INSTRUMENT LANDING SYSTEM (ILS) PROVIDES AZIMUTH, DISTANCE, ELEVATION, AND GLIDE PATH POSITION TO AIRCRAFT ON A PRECISION APPROACH TO THE ILS INSTRUMENTED RUNWAY. THE ILS OPERATES IN THE VHF AND UHF RADIO BANDS.

title: MICROW LAND SYS
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: THE MICROWAVE LANDING SYSTEM (MLS) PROVIDES AZIMUTH, DISTANCE, ELEVATION, AND GLIDE PATH POSITION TO AIRCRAFT ON A PRECISION APPROACH TO THE MLS INSTRUMENTED RUNWAY. THE MLS OPERATES IN A NARROW BAND MICROWAVE FREQUENCY.
description: THE MICROWAVE LANDING SYSTEM (MLS) PROVIDES AZIMUTH, DISTANCE, ELEVATION, AND GLIDE PATH POSITION TO AIRCRAFT ON A PRECISION APPROACH TO THE MLS INSTRUMENTED RUNWAY. THE MLS OPERATES IN A NARROW BAND MICROWAVE FREQUENCY.

title: SHORE AUTOM CARR LAND SYS
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: THE SHORE-BASED AUTOMATIC CARRIER LANDING SYSTEM (ACLS) IS AN UNATTENDED, SELF-CONTAINED RADAR SYSTEM. THE ACLS CONSISTS OF PRECISION TRACKING RADAR COUPLED TO A COMPUTER DATA LINK TO PROVIDE CONTINUOUS INFORMATION TO THE AIRCRAFT, MONITORING CAPABILITY TO THE PILOT, AND A BACKUP APPROACH SYSTEM.

THE ACLS CONSISTS OF PRECISION TRACKING RADAR COUPLED TO A COMPUTER DATA LINK TO PROVIDE CONTINUOUS INFORMATION TO THE AIRCRAFT, MONITORING CAPABILITY TO THE PILOT, AND A BACKUP APPROACH SYSTEM.

title: OBSTRUCTION LTG / MARKINGS
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: OBSTRUCTION LIGHTING PROVIDES VISUAL IDENTIFICATION OF OBJECTS AT NIGHT, OR IN SOME CASES IN DAY TIMES, THAT ARE POTENTIALLY HAZARDOUS TO AIR NAVIGATION.
description: OBSTRUCTION LIGHTING PROVIDES VISUAL IDENTIFICATION OF OBJECTS AT NIGHT, OR IN SOME CASES IN DAY TIMES, THAT ARE POTENTIALLY HAZARDOUS TO AIR NAVIGATION.

title: VISUAL APPR SLOPE INDICTR
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: THE VISUAL APPROACH SLOPE INDICATOR (VASI) SYSTEM IS AN UNATTENDED SYSTEM THAT PROVIDES VISUAL GLIDE SLOPE GUIDANCE TO PILOTS OF AIRCRAFT DURING THE FINAL LANDING APPROACH. THE VASI IS HELPFUL DURING DAY AND NIGHT OPERATIONS AND FOR VISUAL FLIGHT RULES (VFR) AND INSTRUMENT FLIGHT RULES (IFR) OPERATIONS.
description: THE VISUAL APPROACH SLOPE INDICATOR (VASI) SYSTEM IS AN UNATTENDED SYSTEM THAT PROVIDES VISUAL GLIDE SLOPE GUIDANCE TO PILOTS OF AIRCRAFT DURING THE FINAL LANDING APPROACH. THE VASI IS HELPFUL DURING DAY AND NIGHT OPERATIONS AND FOR VISUAL FLIGHT RULES (VFR) AND INSTRUMENT FLIGHT RULES (IFR) OPERATIONS.

title: PREC APPR PATH IND SYS
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: THE PRECISION APPROACH PATH INDICATOR (PAPI) SYSTEM IS AN UNATTENDED SYSTEM WHICH PROVIDES VISUAL GLIDE SLOPE GUIDANCE TO PILOTS OF AIRCRAFT DURING THE FINAL LANDING APPROACH. THE PAPI SYSTEM PROVIDES THIS INFORMATION DURING THE DAY AND NIGHT FOR VISUAL FLIGHT RULES (VFR) AND INSTRUMENT FLIGHT RULES (IFR) OPERATIONS AS LOW AS CATEGORY I CONDITIONS.
description: THE PRECISION APPROACH PATH INDICATOR (PAPI) SYSTEM IS AN UNATTENDED SYSTEM WHICH PROVIDES VISUAL GLIDE SLOPE GUIDANCE TO PILOTS OF AIRCRAFT DURING THE FINAL LANDING APPROACH. THE PAPI SYSTEM PROVIDES THIS INFORMATION DURING THE DAY AND NIGHT FOR VISUAL FLIGHT RULES (VFR) AND INSTRUMENT FLIGHT RULES (IFR) OPERATIONS AS LOW AS CATEGORY I CONDITIONS.

title: OPT LANDING AIDS
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: OPTICAL LANDING AIDS PROVIDE THE PILOT APPROACHING FOR A LANDING WITH A VISUAL SIGNAL TO ASSIST IN INTERCEPTING AND MAINTAINING THE CORRECT APPROACH GLIDE SLOPE. THE OLA IS A REQUIRED VISUAL AID FOR LANDINGS ON AIRCRAFT CARRIERS, BUT ON SHORE-BASED AIRFIELDS THE OLA IS PRIMARILY AN AID FOR TRAINING OR PRACTICE. THE OLA MAY BE USED DURING DAY OR NIGHT OPERATIONS AND IN ALL WEATHER CONDITIONS.
description: OPTICAL LANDING AIDS PROVIDE THE PILOT APPROACHING FOR A LANDING WITH A VISUAL SIGNAL TO ASSIST IN INTERCEPTING AND MAINTAINING THE CORRECT APPROACH GLIDE SLOPE. THE OLA IS A REQUIRED VISUAL AID FOR LANDINGS ON AIRCRAFT CARRIERS, BUT ON SHORE-BASED AIRFIELDS THE OLA IS PRIMARILY AN AID FOR TRAINING OR PRACTICE. THE OLA MAY BE USED DURING DAY OR NIGHT OPERATIONS AND IN ALL WEATHER CONDITIONS.

title: WIND DIRECTION INDICATOR
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: A WIND DIRECTION INDICATOR PROVIDES VISUAL INFORMATION OF THE SURFACE WIND DIRECTION AND GENERAL INDICATION OF THE WIND SPEED TO THE AIRCREW. THIS WIND INFORMATION IS MOST USEFUL DURING TAKEOFF, FOR ORIENTATION TO MAKE AN APPROACH, AND IN THE FINAL PHASE OF APPROACH PRIOR TO TOUCHDOWN.
description: A WIND DIRECTION INDICATOR PROVIDES VISUAL INFORMATION OF THE SURFACE WIND DIRECTION AND GENERAL INDICATION OF THE WIND SPEED TO THE AIRCREW. THIS WIND INFORMATION IS MOST USEFUL DURING TAKEOFF, FOR ORIENTATION TO MAKE AN APPROACH, AND IN THE FINAL PHASE OF APPROACH PRIOR TO TOUCHDOWN.

title: RUNWAY DISTANCE MARKERS
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: THE PURPOSE OF RUNWAY DISTANCE MARKERS IS TO INDICATE TO AIRCREWS THE DISTANCE REMAINING TO THE END OF THE RUNWAY DURING TAKEOFF AND LANDING. THE RDM PROVIDE THIS INFORMATION FOR DAY AND NIGHT OPERATIONS IN ALL WEATHER CONDITIONS. THE RDM SHOULD BE PROVIDED FOR ALL RUNWAYS WHERE FIXED WING JET AIRCRAFT OPS ARE CONDUCTED AND ARE RECOMMENDED FOR RUNWAYS INTENDED FOR OPS OF PROPELLER TYPE AIRCRAFT.
description: THE PURPOSE OF RUNWAY DISTANCE MARKERS IS TO INDICATE TO AIRCREWS THE DISTANCE REMAINING TO THE END OF THE RUNWAY DURING TAKEOFF AND LANDING. THE RDM PROVIDE THIS INFORMATION FOR DAY AND NIGHT OPERATIONS IN ALL WEATHER CONDITIONS. THE RDM SHOULD BE PROVIDED FOR ALL RUNWAYS WHERE FIXED WING JET AIRCRAFT OPS ARE CONDUCTED AND ARE RECOMMENDED FOR RUNWAYS INTENDED FOR OPS OF PROPELLER TYPE AIRCRAFT.

title: VOR/TACAN CHK SIGN
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: A VOR/TACAN CHECK SIGN PROVIDES INFORMATION FOR THE PILOT WHEN VERIFYING THE OPERATION OF THE NAVIGATIONAL AID IN THE AIRCRAFT BEFORE TAKING OFF. THIS CHECK SIGN IS A VISUAL IDENTIFICATION MARKER ERECTED IN THE AREA ADJACENT TO THE AIRCRAFT HOLDING POINT AT THE TAXIWAY ACCESS TO RUNWAY ENDS.
description: A VOR/TACAN CHECK SIGN PROVIDES INFORMATION FOR THE PILOT WHEN VERIFYING THE OPERATION OF THE NAVIGATIONAL AID IN THE AIRCRAFT BEFORE TAKING OFF. THIS CHECK SIGN IS A VISUAL IDENTIFICATION MARKER ERECTED IN THE AREA ADJACENT TO THE AIRCRAFT HOLDING POINT AT THE TAXIWAY ACCESS TO RUNWAY ENDS.

title: RADAR TOWER
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: RADAR TOWERS ARE TOWERS HOSTING VARIOUS RADAR SYSTEMS AND MUST BE PLANNED ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS.
description: RADAR TOWERS ARE TOWERS HOSTING VARIOUS RADAR SYSTEMS AND MUST BE PLANNED ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS.

title: AVIATION METEORLOGICAL
subCategory: 134 NAV & TRAFFIC AIDS - OTHER Basic Category Code group 134 applies to structures which function as aircraft navigation/ traffic aids.
notes: WEATHER FORECASTING FACILITY THAT SUPPORTS AIR OPERATIONS.
description: WEATHER FORECASTING FACILITY THAT SUPPORTS AIR OPERATIONS.

title: COMM LINES EXCL TELEPHONE
subCategory: 135 COMM & CONTROL LINES Communication lines provide circuits between the various activities on or off the station.
notes: COMMUNICATION LINES PROVIDE CIRCUITS BETWEEN THE VARIOUS ACTIVITIES ON OR OFF THE STATION. THE COMMUNICATIONS NET MAY INCLUDE TRUNK LINE SERVICE CABLE, FEEDER LINES, AND DIRECT CIRCUITS DEPENDING ON THE COMPLEXITY OF THE SYSTEM.
description: COMMUNICATION LINES PROVIDE CIRCUITS BETWEEN THE VARIOUS ACTIVITIES ON OR OFF THE STATION. THE COMMUNICATIONS NET MAY INCLUDE TRUNK LINE SERVICE CABLE, FEEDER LINES, AND DIRECT CIRCUITS DEPENDING ON THE COMPLEXITY OF THE SYSTEM.

title: TELEPHONE LINES
subCategory: 135 COMM & CONTROL LINES Communication lines provide circuits between the various activities on or off the station.
notes: COMMUNICATION LINES, TO INCLUDE OVERHEAD, UNDERGROUND, AND MARINE CABLES AND LINES.
description: COMMUNICATION LINES, TO INCLUDE OVERHEAD, UNDERGROUND, AND MARINE CABLES AND LINES.

title: APPROACH LIGHTING
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: APPROACH LIGHTING ENHANCES THE AIRCREW'S ABILITY TO ACQUIRE THE RUNWAY ENVIRONMENT VISUALLY WHEN MAKING AN APPROACH FOR LANDING DURING PERIODS OF REDUCED VISIBILITY. VISUAL CUES FOR DIRECTIONAL AND ROLL GUIDANCE ARE PROVIDED TO THE AIRCREW FOR OPERATIONS AT NIGHT AND IN MARGINAL WEATHER CONDITIONS BY DAY. THE SYSTEM INCLUDES BOTH APPROACH LIGHTS AND SEQUENCED FLASHERS.
description: APPROACH LIGHTING ENHANCES THE AIRCREW'S ABILITY TO ACQUIRE THE RUNWAY ENVIRONMENT VISUALLY WHEN MAKING AN APPROACH FOR LANDING DURING PERIODS OF REDUCED VISIBILITY. VISUAL CUES FOR DIRECTIONAL AND ROLL GUIDANCE ARE PROVIDED TO THE AIRCREW FOR OPERATIONS AT NIGHT AND IN MARGINAL WEATHER CONDITIONS BY DAY. THE SYSTEM INCLUDES BOTH APPROACH LIGHTS AND SEQUENCED FLASHERS.

title: APRON & PKG APRN LIGHTING
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: APRON AND PARKING AREA LIGHTING ENABLES THE AIRCREW TO GUIDE THEIR AIRCRAFT INTO POSITION FOR LOADING, SERVICING, OR PARKING AND PROVIDES ILLUMINATION TO PERFORM SUCH FUNCTIONS AS FUELING, MAINTENANCE, LOADING, UNLOADING, AND SECURITY.
description: APRON AND PARKING AREA LIGHTING ENABLES THE AIRCREW TO GUIDE THEIR AIRCRAFT INTO POSITION FOR LOADING, SERVICING, OR PARKING AND PROVIDES ILLUMINATION TO PERFORM SUCH FUNCTIONS AS FUELING, MAINTENANCE, LOADING, UNLOADING, AND SECURITY.

title: RUNWAY EDGE LIGHTING
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: THIS CATEGORY CODE INCLUDES TWO GROUPS OF LIGHTS, RUNWAY EDGE LIGHTS, WHICH DEFINE THE LATERAL LIMITS OF THE PAVEMENT, AND CIRCLING GUIDANCE LIGHTS (CGLS), WHICH ENABLE AN AIRBORNE AIRCREW TO LOCATE THE RUNWAY WHILE OFF TO THE SIDE OF THE RUNWAY, AND ESTABLISH THE PROPER TRAFFIC PATTERN.
description: THIS CATEGORY CODE INCLUDES TWO GROUPS OF LIGHTS, RUNWAY EDGE LIGHTS, WHICH DEFINE THE LATERAL LIMITS OF THE PAVEMENT, AND CIRCLING GUIDANCE LIGHTS (CGLS), WHICH ENABLE AN AIRBORNE AIRCREW TO LOCATE THE RUNWAY WHILE OFF TO THE SIDE OF THE RUNWAY, AND ESTABLISH THE PROPER TRAFFIC PATTERN.

title: RUNWAY CENTERLINE LIGHTING
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: RUNWAY CENTERLINE LIGHTING PROVIDES VISUAL AID TO ASSIST THE AIRCREW IN KEEPING THE AIRCRAFT CENTERED ON THE RUNWAY DURING TAKE-OFF AND AFTER LANDING AT NIGHT OR IN CONDITION OF REDUCED VISIBILITY. IT IS A SUPPLEMENT TO RUNWAY EDGE LIGHTING AND MARKINGS AND CIRCLING GUIDANCE LIGHTING, CATEGORY CODE 136 30.
description: RUNWAY CENTERLINE LIGHTING PROVIDES VISUAL AID TO ASSIST THE AIRCREW IN KEEPING THE AIRCRAFT CENTERED ON THE RUNWAY DURING TAKE-OFF AND AFTER LANDING AT NIGHT OR IN CONDITION OF REDUCED VISIBILITY. IT IS A SUPPLEMENT TO RUNWAY EDGE LIGHTING AND MARKINGS AND CIRCLING GUIDANCE LIGHTING, CATEGORY CODE 136 30.

title: SIMULATE CARRIER DECK LTG
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: A SIMULATED CARRIER DECK IS USED TO TRAIN PILOTS ASHORE FOR LANDING AIRCRAFT UNDER SIMULATED CONDITIONS OF A CARRIER AT SEA. SIMULATED CARRIER DECK LIGHTING AND MARKINGS PERMITS TRAINING DURING THE DAY, NIGHT, AND ADVERSE VISIBILITY CONDITIONS.
description: A SIMULATED CARRIER DECK IS USED TO TRAIN PILOTS ASHORE FOR LANDING AIRCRAFT UNDER SIMULATED CONDITIONS OF A CARRIER AT SEA. SIMULATED CARRIER DECK LIGHTING AND MARKINGS PERMITS TRAINING DURING THE DAY, NIGHT, AND ADVERSE VISIBILITY CONDITIONS.

title: WHEELS UP / WAVE OFF LTG
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: WHEELS-UP AND WAVE-OFF LIGHTS ARE PROVIDED TO ALLOW EITHER THE WHEEL WATCH, THE LANDING SIGNAL OFFICER (LSO), OR CONTROL TOWER PERSONNEL TO DETERMINE IF A LANDING AIRCRAFT HAS ITS LANDING GEAR FULLY EXTENDED AND/OR TO SIGNAL TO AN AIRCREW TO ABORT OR 'WAVE-OFF' A LANDING ATTEMPT.
description: WHEELS-UP AND WAVE-OFF LIGHTS ARE PROVIDED TO ALLOW EITHER THE WHEEL WATCH, THE LANDING SIGNAL OFFICER (LSO), OR CONTROL TOWER PERSONNEL TO DETERMINE IF A LANDING AIRCRAFT HAS ITS LANDING GEAR FULLY EXTENDED AND/OR TO SIGNAL TO AN AIRCREW TO ABORT OR 'WAVE-OFF' A LANDING ATTEMPT.

title: TAXIWAY LIGHTING
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: TAXIWAY LIGHTING AND MARKINGS DEFINE THE LATERAL LIMITS AND DIRECTION OF A TAXIWAY, CATEGORY CODE 112-10, TO GUIDE AIRCRAFT MOVEMENT BETWEEN THE RUNWAY OPERATIONAL AREA AND THE AIRCRAFT PARKING AREA DURING NIGHT OPERATIONS OR CONDITIONS OF POOR VISIBILITY.
description: TAXIWAY LIGHTING AND MARKINGS DEFINE THE LATERAL LIMITS AND DIRECTION OF A TAXIWAY, CATEGORY CODE 112-10, TO GUIDE AIRCRAFT MOVEMENT BETWEEN THE RUNWAY OPERATIONAL AREA AND THE AIRCRAFT PARKING AREA DURING NIGHT OPERATIONS OR CONDITIONS OF POOR VISIBILITY.

title: TOUCHDOWN ZONE LIGHTING
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: TOUCHDOWN ZONE LIGHTING DELINEATES THE TOUCHDOWN ZONE AND PROVIDES DIRECTIONAL AND ROLL GUIDANCE FOR AIRCRAFT APPROACHING THE THRESHOLD. IT PROVIDES VISUAL CUES FOR MORE ACCURATELY CENTERING THE AIRCRAFT ON THE RUNWAY, ADJUSTING ATTITUDE FOR TOUCHDOWN, AND DETERMINING THE TOUCHDOWN POSITION. THE LIGHTING CONSISTS OF BARS OF WHITE LIGHTS IN THE PAVEMENT ON EACH SIDE OF THE RUNWAY CENTERLINE.
description: TOUCHDOWN ZONE LIGHTING DELINEATES THE TOUCHDOWN ZONE AND PROVIDES DIRECTIONAL AND ROLL GUIDANCE FOR AIRCRAFT APPROACHING THE THRESHOLD. IT PROVIDES VISUAL CUES FOR MORE ACCURATELY CENTERING THE AIRCRAFT ON THE RUNWAY, ADJUSTING ATTITUDE FOR TOUCHDOWN, AND DETERMINING THE TOUCHDOWN POSITION. THE LIGHTING CONSISTS OF BARS OF WHITE LIGHTS IN THE PAVEMENT ON EACH SIDE OF THE RUNWAY CENTERLINE.

title: THRESHOLD LIGHTING
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: THRESHOLD LIGHTING IS A SYSTEM OF LIGHTS DEFINING THE ENDS OF THE USABLE RUNWAY SURFACE. THEY INCLUDE THRESHOLD LIGHTS, DISPLACED THRESHOLD LIGHTS, RUNWAY END IDENTIFICATION LIGHTS (REIL) AND RUNWAY END LIGHTS. THE THRESHOLD LIGHTS ARE DISPLACED FROM THE EXTREMITY OF THE RUNWAY WHEN A PORTION IS UNAVAILABLE FOR NORMAL OPERATIONS
description: THRESHOLD LIGHTING IS A SYSTEM OF LIGHTS DEFINING THE ENDS OF THE USABLE RUNWAY SURFACE. THEY INCLUDE THRESHOLD LIGHTS, DISPLACED THRESHOLD LIGHTS, RUNWAY END IDENTIFICATION LIGHTS (REIL) AND RUNWAY END LIGHTS. THE THRESHOLD LIGHTS ARE DISPLACED FROM THE EXTREMITY OF THE RUNWAY WHEN A PORTION IS UNAVAILABLE FOR NORMAL OPERATIONS

title: HELIPORT LIGHTING
subCategory: 136 AIRFIELD PAVEMENT LIGHTING Airfield pavement lighting includes facilities for lighting all airfield pavements and approaches including airfield Visual Landing Aids for approaches, landings, takeoffs, taxiing, and surface maneuvering of aircraft on Navy and Marine Corps airfields. The visual landing aids include lighting and markings.
notes: HELIPORT LIGHTING IS A SYSTEM OF LIGHTS ARRANGED TO CLEARLY DEFINE THE HELICOPTER LANDING PAD FOR OPERATIONS AT NIGHT AND DURING PERIODS OF POOR VISIBILITY. HELIPORT LIGHTING INCLUDES ALL VISUAL REFERENCE ASPECTS OF THE APPROACH AND LANDING OF ROTARY WING AIRCRAFT. THIS INCLUDES VISUAL AIDS, MARKINGS, PERIMETER AND APPROACH LIGHTS, AND RUNWAY AND TAXIWAY LIGHTS.
description: HELIPORT LIGHTING IS A SYSTEM OF LIGHTS ARRANGED TO CLEARLY DEFINE THE HELICOPTER LANDING PAD FOR OPERATIONS AT NIGHT AND DURING PERIODS OF POOR VISIBILITY. HELIPORT LIGHTING INCLUDES ALL VISUAL REFERENCE ASPECTS OF THE APPROACH AND LANDING OF ROTARY WING AIRCRAFT. THIS INCLUDES VISUAL AIDS, MARKINGS, PERIMETER AND APPROACH LIGHTS, AND RUNWAY AND TAXIWAY LIGHTS.

title: LIGHTHOUSE
subCategory: 137 SHIP NAV&TRAFFIC AIDS-BLDG This code group applies to buildings for housing sea traffic control, navigation aids and navigation services.
notes: A LIGHTHOUSE IS A BUILDING THAT HOUSES A NAVIGATION BEACON THAT MAY EMIT LIGHT, SOUND, RADIO, RADAR, OR A COMBINATION THEREOF. IT MAY BE ONSHORE OR OFFSHORE. CONSTRUCTION IS DONE OVERSEAS WHEN APPROPRIATE BY THE NAVAL FACILITIES ENGINEERING COMMAND.
description: A LIGHTHOUSE IS A BUILDING THAT HOUSES A NAVIGATION BEACON THAT MAY EMIT LIGHT, SOUND, RADIO, RADAR, OR A COMBINATION THEREOF. IT MAY BE ONSHORE OR OFFSHORE. CONSTRUCTION IS DONE OVERSEAS WHEN APPROPRIATE BY THE NAVAL FACILITIES ENGINEERING COMMAND.

title: BEACON-SHIP
subCategory: 138 SHIP NAV&TRAFFIC AIDS-OTHR This code group applies to structures which function as sea traffic navigation/traffic aids.
notes: THE U.S. COAST GUARD HAS SPECIFIC JURISDICTION OVER ALL AIDS TO NAVIGATION (DAY BEACONS, BUOYS, FOGHORNS, ETC.,) IN THE CONTINENTAL UNITED STATES AND IN ALL OUTLYING TERRITORIES AND POSSESSIONS. DAY BEACONS ARE UNLIGHTED STRUCTURES USED TO MARK ISOLATED DANGERS OR CHANNELS, EDGES, OR ALIGNMENT.

DAY BEACONS ARE UNLIGHTED STRUCTURES USED TO MARK ISOLATED DANGERS OR CHANNELS, EDGES, OR ALIGNMENT.

title: AIR PASSENGER TERMINAL
subCategory: 141 OPERATIONAL - BUILDINGS All buildings, which are non-ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: THE AIR PASSENGER TERMINAL PROVIDES FACILITIES FOR PROCESSING AUTHORIZED PASSENGERS AND THEIR BAGGAGE AND FOR PROCESSING INCIDENTAL FREIGHT. SPACE IS PROVIDED IN THE TERMINAL FOR THE FOLLOWING FUNCTIONAL AREAS: ADMINISTRATIVE SPACE, BAGGAGE CLAIM ROOM, CHECK-IN COUNTER, MINOR FREIGHT STORAGE, INFORMATION COUNTER, AND WAITING LOUNGE WITH FOOD CONCESSIONS.

title: AIR CARGO TERMINAL
subCategory: 141 OPERATIONAL - BUILDINGS All buildings, which are non-ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: AN AIR CARGO TERMINAL IS PLANNED FOR AIR STATIONS WHERE CARGO AND FREIGHT HANDLING EXCEEDS 10,000 POUNDS PER DAY. THE AIR CARGO TERMINAL IS SEPARATE FROM THE AIR PASSENGER TERMINAL WHERE ONLY INCIDENTAL FREIGHT IS HANDLED. AIR CARGO TERMINAL FUNCTIONS INCLUDE RECEIPT OF PACKAGES, CONTROL DOCUMENTATION, PALLETIZATION, HOLDING FOR SHIPMENT, AIRCRAFT LOADING AND UNLOADING.
description: AN AIR CARGO TERMINAL IS PLANNED FOR AIR STATIONS WHERE CARGO AND FREIGHT HANDLING EXCEEDS 10,000 POUNDS PER DAY. title: COURIER STATION
subCategory: 141 OPERATIONAL - BUILDINGS All buildings, which are non-ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: THIS FACILITY IS USED TO STORE AND DISSEMINATE CLASSIFIED MATERIAL FOR MISSION PLANNING, PILOT TRAINING AND BRIEFINGS IN SUPPORT OF ATTACK AIRCRAFT OPERATIONS.
description: THIS FACILITY IS USED TO STORE AND DISSEMINATE CLASSIFIED MATERIAL FOR MISSION PLANNING, PILOT TRAINING AND BRIEFINGS IN SUPPORT OF ATTACK AIRCRAFT OPERATIONS.

title: ACFT FIRE AND RESCUE STA
subCategory: 141 OPERATIONAL - BUILDINGS All buildings, which are non-ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: THE AIRCRAFT FIRE AND RESCUE STATION PROVIDES FIRE AND EMERGENCY RESCUE PROTECTION FOR PILOTS AND AIRCRAFT. WHEN FEASIBLE, THE AIRCRAFT FIRE AND RESCUE STATION IS COMBINED WITH THE STRUCTURAL FIRE STATION (CATEGORY CODE 730 10) TO FORM ONE COMPLETE EMERGENCY FACILITY, CATEGORY CODE 141 25, COMBINED STRUCTURAL/ AIRCRAFT FIRE/RESCUE STATION.
description: THE AIRCRAFT FIRE AND RESCUE STATION PROVIDES FIRE AND EMERGENCY RESCUE PROTECTION FOR PILOTS AND AIRCRAFT. title: COMBINED FIRE/RESCUE STA
subCategory: 141 OPERATIONAL - BUILDINGS All buildings, which are non-ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: A COMBINED STRUCTURAL/AIRCRAFT FIRE/RESCUE STATION IS PLANNED UNDER CERTAIN CONDITIONS TO SERVE THE FUNCTION OF A STRUCTURAL FIRE STATION (CATEGORY CODE 730 10) AND AN AIRCRAFT FIRE AND RESCUE STATIONS. FIRE AND RESCUE STATION. THE COMBINED FACILITY IS PLANNED FOR A LOCATION THAT SATISFIES THE RESPONSE TIME AND DISTANCE REQUIREMENTS FOR BOTH THE STRUCTURAL FIRE AND THE AIRCRAFT FIRE.

title: ACFT LINE OPERATIONS BLDG

THE BUILDING IS UTILIZED IN KEEPING OF SQUADRON DAILY FLIGHT BOOKS, AIRCRAFT STATUS BOARDS, AND BULLETIN BOARDS AND AS SUPPORT FOR LINE OPERATIONS PERSONNEL BY PROVIDING SHELTER, A WATER COOLER, AND A CHEMICAL TOILET.

title: ACFT OPS BLDG *EXC 141-70*

notes: AN AIRCRAFT OPERATIONS BUILDING IS PLANNED FOR ALL NAVY AIR STATIONS, AUXILIARY AIR STATIONS, AND AIR FACILITIES. THE BUILDING HOUSES THE ADMINISTRATION OF FLIGHT OPERATIONAL ACTIVITIES WITH ALL SUPPORTING FUNCTIONS INCLUDING FLIGHT CONTROL, COMMUNICATIONS, AND WEATHER SERVICES.
description: AN AIRCRAFT OPERATIONS BUILDING IS PLANNED FOR ALL NAVY AIR STATIONS, AUXILIARY AIR STATIONS, AND AIR FACILITIES. THE BUILDING HOUSES THE ADMINISTRATION OF FLIGHT OPERATIONAL ACTIVITIES WITH ALL SUPPORTING FUNCTIONS INCLUDING FLIGHT CONTROL, COMMUNICATIONS, AND WEATHER SERVICES.

notes: THE MATCU PERFORMS A COMBINED FUNCTION SIMILAR TO THAT ACCOMPLISHED WITH GROUND CONTROL APPROACH (GCA) SYSTEM; TACAN BUILDING; AND, AIR SURVEILLANCE RADAR (ASR) BUILDING. DEPENDING ON THE LEVEL OF AIRCRAFT OPERATIONS, THE MATCU OPERATIONS BUILDING MAY PROVIDE THE SOLE GCA SUPPORT AT AN AIR INSTALLATION OR MAY SUPPLEMENT AND BE IN ADDITION TO PERMANENT ASR, TACAN, AND GCA FACILITIES
description: THE MATCU PERFORMS A COMBINED FUNCTION SIMILAR TO THAT ACCOMPLISHED WITH GROUND CONTROL APPROACH (GCA) SYSTEM; TACAN BUILDING; AND, AIR SURVEILLANCE RADAR (ASR) BUILDING. title: CONTROL TWR ATTD/FREE STD

IT IS AN ELEVATED BUILDING HAVING AN UNOBSTRUCTED LINE-OF SIGHT TO THE AIRFIELD APPROACH AREAS, RUNWAYS, TAXIWAYS, AIRCRAFT PARKING AREAS, AND ALL OTHER AREAS OVER WHICH AIRCRAFT MOVEMENTS MUST BE CONTROLLED.

title: GCA CREW FACILITY

notes: THE GROUND CONTROL APPROACH (GCA) CREW FACILITY PROVIDES A READY ROOM FOR ON DUTY PERSONNEL ASSIGNED TO THE GCA VAN. THE FACILITY CONSISTS OF TWO (2) STANDARD DESIGN SKID-MOUNTED SHELTERS (EACH 12 FEET BY 20 FEET). THE CREW FACILITY IS AUTHORIZED WHENEVER THE MOBILE GCA UNIT IS FURNISHED.

notes: PRESSURE SUIT MAINTENANCE PERFORMED UNDER THIS CATCODE, NORMALLY IN THE PARACHUTE AND SURVIVAL EQUIPMENT SHOP (CATEGORY CODE 211 34). SPECIAL JUSTIFICATION IS REQUIRED TO PROVIDE A SEPARATE FACILITY FOR THIS PURPOSE.
description: PRESSURE SUIT MAINTENANCE PERFORMED UNDER THIS CATCODE, NORMALLY IN THE PARACHUTE AND SURVIVAL EQUIPMENT SHOP (CATEGORY CODE 211 34). SPECIAL JUSTIFICATION IS REQUIRED TO PROVIDE A SEPARATE FACILITY FOR THIS PURPOSE.

title: LIQUID OXY/NIT FAC(NONIND)
subCategory: 141 OPERATIONAL - BUILDINGS All buildings, which are non-ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: A LIQUID OXYGEN/NITROGEN FACILITY IS REQUIRED AT EACH NAVY AND MARINE CORPS AIR STATION WHERE 50 OR MORE ATTACK AND FIGHTER-TYPE AIRCRAFT ATE ASSIGNED. THE FACILITY PROVIDES FOR STORAGE, VAPORIZATION, AND TRANSFER OF NON- INDUSTRIAL OXYGEN AND NITROGEN AND FOR TEST AND REPAIR OF CRYOGENIC EQUIPMENT ASSOCIATED WITH AVIATOR AND AIRCRAFT SUPPORT.

notes: THIS IS A SHELTER FOR OFFICIAL EMERGENCY AND ALERT VEHICLES, SUCH AS AN AMBULANCE. IT IS JUSTIFIED IN INSTANCES WHEN IMMEDIATE RESPONSE REQUIRED BY SPECIAL WATERFRONT OPERATIONAL VEHICLE.
description: THIS IS A SHELTER FOR OFFICIAL EMERGENCY AND ALERT VEHICLES, SUCH AS AN AMBULANCE. IT IS JUSTIFIED IN INSTANCES WHEN IMMEDIATE RESPONSE REQUIRED BY SPECIAL WATERFRONT OPERATIONAL VEHICLE.

title: OPERATIONAL VEHICLE GARAGE
subCategory: 143 SHIP & OTHER OPERTNL-BLDGS All buildings, which are ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: AN OPERATIONAL VEHICLE GARAGE IS USED FOR THE STORAGE OF VEHICLES WHICH ARE NOT UTILIZED ON A DAILY BASIS AND WHICH ARE EXPOSED TO ADVERSE WEATHER CONDITIONS THAT WOULD HAVE A DETRIMENTAL EFFECT UPON THEM IF STORED OUT IN THE OPEN. ACCORDINGLY, THE TYPE OF VEHICLE STORED, FREQUENCY OF USE AND CLIMATIC CONDITIONS, WILL DETERMINE WHETHER THIS TYPE OF FACILITY IS WARRANTED.

ACCORDINGLY, THE TYPE OF VEHICLE STORED, FREQUENCY OF USE AND CLIMATIC CONDITIONS, WILL DETERMINE WHETHER THIS TYPE OF FACILITY IS WARRANTED.

title: RANGE OPERATIONS CENTER
subCategory: 143 SHIP & OTHER OPERTNL-BLDGS All buildings, which are ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: A RANGE OPERATIONS CENTER IS THE CONTROL POINT FOR TESTING TORPEDOES, CALIBRATING SHIPS' FIRING SYSTEMS, AND TRAINING PILOTS AND TESTING AIRCRAFT ON GUNNERY AND BOMBING RANGES. THE CENTER WILL VARY WITH THE EQUIPMENT AND CONTROL AREAS REQUIRED. STANDARD PLANNING FACTORS FOR A CENTER ARE NOT AVAILABLE. ITS SIZE MUST BE PLANNED TO SUPPORT THE EQUIPMENT AND CONTROL AREAS TO BE HOUSED.
description: A RANGE OPERATIONS CENTER IS THE CONTROL POINT FOR TESTING TORPEDOES, CALIBRATING SHIPS' FIRING SYSTEMS, AND TRAINING PILOTS AND TESTING AIRCRAFT ON GUNNERY AND BOMBING RANGES. title: ORDNANCE OPERATIONS BLDG

title: ALERT FORCE BUILDING
subCategory: 143 SHIP & OTHER OPERTNL-BLDGS All buildings, which are ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: AN ALERT FORCE BUILDING IS PROGRAMMED IN CONJUNCTION WITH AN AIR/UNDERWATER WEAPONS SHOP, WHEN REQUIRED TO MEET THE ALERT FORCE RESPONSE TIMES ESTABLISHED FOR THE SHOP. THE ALERT FORCE BUILDING BARRACKS FACILITIES, INCLUDING LIMITED FACILITIES NECESSARY TO SUPPORT THE GUARD OF THE DAY FOR AN AUW SHOP. IT ALSO CONTAINS A DUTY OFFICE, PROVISION FOR WEAPONS STORAGE AND AN ALARM REPEATER PANEL.

title: TRANSIT SHED
subCategory: 143 SHIP & OTHER OPERTNL-BLDGS All buildings, which are ship related, used for housing operational types of activities and equipment
notes: A TRANSIT SHED IS PLANNED TO SUPPORT THE RAPID AND ORDERLY TRANSFER OF TRUCK AND RAIL FREIGHT IN SHIPMENT FROM ONE CARRIER TO ANOTHER WITH MINIMUM STORAGE. FOR A WATERFRONT TRANSIT SHED, SEE CATEGORY CODE 156 10. THE TRANSIT SHED IS OF THE MINIMUM DESIGN THAT WILL PROTECT THE FREIGHT FROM THE WEATHER AND PROVIDE ANY SECURITY NECESSARY.

THIS FACILITY CAN ALSO BE DESIGNATED AS A REGIONAL OPERATIONS CENTER (ROC), EMERGENCY CONTROL CENTER (ECC), OR EMERGENCY ACTION CENTER (EAC).

title: MISSION OPS COMM/CONTR FAC

AIRLANT, SUBPAC), OPERATIONAL SUPPORT COMMANDS (E.G. CTF AND CTG) AND A MARITIME OPERATIONS CENTER (MOC).

title: ORDNANCE DEMOLITION AREA

title: EXPLOSIVE TRUCK HOLDG YD
subCategory: 148 SHIP OPRTNL FAC- This category group contains facilities and structures which support tactical or organizational ship and other land operations in which do not fall readily into another category. For facilities supporting aircraft operations, use category group 149.
notes: THIS YARD IS WHERE TRUCKS CONTAINING AMMUNITION AND/OR EXPLOSIVES ARE HELD FOR INTERIM PERIODS OF TIME PRIOR TO STORAGE OR SHIPMENT. SAFE HAVENS AND WHARF YARDS NEAR PIERS AND WHARVES SHOULD BE CATEGORIZED UNDER THIS FUNCTION. EACH FACILITY REQUIRES INDIVIDUAL JUSTIFICATION. DEPENDING ON LAND CONSTRAINTS AND EXPLOSIVE SAFETY CRITERIA THESE FACILITIES MAY OR MAY NOT BE BARRICADED.

notes: THIS IS AN OPEN AREA THAT PROVIDES A TEMPORARY HOLDING OR STAGING AREA FOR CONTAINERS LOADED WITH EXPLOSIVE ORDNANCE. WHARF YARDS NEAR PIERS AND WHARVES ARE ALSO DESCRIBED BY THIS FUNCTION.

title: CONTNR TRANSFER FAC (ORD)

SCALE EQUIPMENT SHOULD BE EXPECTED TO A PART OF THIS FUNCTION. THE AREA MAY BE BARRICADED OR UNBARRICADED.

title: RAIL/TRUCK RECEIVING STA
subCategory: 148 SHIP OPRTNL FAC- This category group contains facilities and structures which support tactical or organizational ship and other land operations in which do not fall readily into another category. For facilities supporting aircraft operations, use category group 149.
notes: A RAIL/TRUCK RECEIVING STATION WEIGHS AND INSPECTS ALL INCOMING SHIPMENTS OF BREAK-BULK AND CONTAINERIZED ORDNANCE ARRIVING BY RAIL OR TRUCK AND ALSO A PERCENTAGE OF THE OUTGOING SHIPMENTS. THIS STATION CAN BE USED AS A SHORT TERM STORAGE FACILITY LIMITED TO OVERNIGHT AND WEEKEND PERIODS AND AS AN INTERCHANGE STORAGE FACILITY LIMITED TO OVERNIGHT AND WEEKEND PERIODS AND AS AN INTERCHANGE YARD.

subCategory: 149 OP FAC OTHER THAN BLDGS This category group contains facilities such as towers and structures which support tactical or organizational aircraft related operations and which do not fall readily into another category. It includes protective construction.
notes: FIXED POINT UTILITIES SYSTEMS SUPPLY UTILITIES TO AIRCRAFT PARKING APRON SERVICE POINTS AND AIRCRAFT MAINTENANCE HANGAR SERVICE POINT. THE FPUS CAN PROVIDE COMPRESSED AIR, PRECONDITIONED AIR FOR HANGARED AIRCRAFT, AND/OR ELECTRICAL POWER. IT CAN CONSIST OF AN ENCLOSED PUMP HOUSE AND STORAGE TANKS, AN IN- GROUND DIST. SYSTEM AND SERVICE POINTS IN AIRCRAFT PARKING APRONS OR AIRCRAFT HANGARS.

THE FPUS CAN PROVIDE COMPRESSED AIR, PRECONDITIONED AIR FOR HANGARED AIRCRAFT, AND/OR ELECTRICAL POWER. IT CAN CONSIST OF AN ENCLOSED PUMP HOUSE AND STORAGE TANKS, AN IN- GROUND DIST. SYSTEM AND SERVICE POINTS IN AIRCRAFT PARKING APRONS OR AIRCRAFT HANGARS.

title: AIRCRAFT CATAPULT
subCategory: 149 OP FAC OTHER THAN BLDGS This category group contains facilities such as towers and structures which support tactical or organizational aircraft related operations and which do not fall readily into another category. It includes protective construction.
notes: THIS IS A FACILITY FOR AIR INSTALLATIONS WHERE SPECIALIZED TRAINING, TEST AND EVALUATION, OR RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT ARE PERFORMED ON CATAPULT TAKEOFFS.
description: THIS IS A FACILITY FOR AIR INSTALLATIONS WHERE SPECIALIZED TRAINING, TEST AND EVALUATION, OR RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT ARE PERFORMED ON CATAPULT TAKEOFFS.

title: AIRCRAFT ARRESTING GEAR
subCategory: 149 OP FAC OTHER THAN BLDGS This category group contains facilities such as towers and structures which support tactical or organizational aircraft related operations and which do not fall readily into another category. It includes protective construction.
notes: AIRCRAFT ARRESTING GEAR IS DESIGNED TO BRING AN AIRCRAFT TO A STOP IN CASE OF AN ABORTED TAKEOFF OR AN EMERGENCY LANDING.
description: AIRCRAFT ARRESTING GEAR IS DESIGNED TO BRING AN AIRCRAFT TO A STOP IN CASE OF AN ABORTED TAKEOFF OR AN EMERGENCY LANDING.

title: MISSILE LAUNCH FACILITY
subCategory: 149 OP FAC OTHER THAN BLDGS This category group contains facilities such as towers and structures which support tactical or organizational aircraft related operations and which do not fall readily into another category. It includes protective construction.
notes: THIS CATEGORY CODE IS FOR LOCATIONS LAUNCHING MISSILES OR DRONES, IT ALSO IS PROVIDED FOR INVENTORY PURPOSES OF MISSILE AND DRONE LAUNCH PADS. SEE NAVSEA OP-5 FOR EXPLOSIVE SAFETY SITING CRITERIA OF ENERGETIC LIQUIDS ASSOCIATED WITH LAUNCH PADS.
description: THIS CATEGORY CODE IS FOR LOCATIONS LAUNCHING MISSILES OR DRONES, IT ALSO IS PROVIDED FOR INVENTORY PURPOSES OF MISSILE AND DRONE LAUNCH PADS. SEE NAVSEA OP-5 FOR EXPLOSIVE SAFETY SITING CRITERIA OF ENERGETIC LIQUIDS ASSOCIATED WITH LAUNCH PADS.

title: BLAST DEFLECTOR FENCE
subCategory: 149 OP FAC OTHER THAN BLDGS This category group contains facilities such as towers and structures which support tactical or organizational aircraft related operations and which do not fall readily into another category. It includes protective construction.
notes: BLAST DEFLECTOR FENCES ARE STRUCTURES THAT DIRECT THE EXHAUST FROM JET ENGINES UPWARD. THEY ARE USED IN CONGESTED AREAS AND PARKING AND MAINTENANCE AREAS TO PROTECT PERSONNEL AND FACILITIES FROM THE BLAST EFFECT OF JET ENGINE EXHAUST. BLAST FENCES ARE ALSO USED TO PREVENT EROSION OF PAVED AND UNPAVED AREAS AND TO PROVIDE PROTECTION FROM FLYING DEBRIS.
description: BLAST DEFLECTOR FENCES ARE STRUCTURES THAT DIRECT THE EXHAUST FROM JET ENGINES UPWARD. THEY ARE USED IN CONGESTED AREAS AND PARKING AND MAINTENANCE AREAS TO PROTECT PERSONNEL AND FACILITIES FROM THE BLAST EFFECT OF JET ENGINE EXHAUST. BLAST FENCES ARE ALSO USED TO PREVENT EROSION OF PAVED AND UNPAVED AREAS AND TO PROVIDE PROTECTION FROM FLYING DEBRIS.

title: EXPEDITIONARY AIR CONTROL
subCategory: 149 OP FAC OTHER THAN BLDGS This category group contains facilities such as towers and structures which support tactical or organizational aircraft related operations and which do not fall readily into another category. It includes protective construction.
notes: THESE ARE MARINE CORPS FACILITIES REQUIRED TO ACCOMMODATE, IN- GARRISON, THE EQUIPMENT USED FOR EXPEDITIONARY AIRCRAFT COMMAND AND CONTROL. THESE FACILITIES ARE ASSIGNED TO SPECIALIZED MARINE CORPS SQUADRONS, AND THE EXPEDITIONARY EQUIPMENT USED IN CONJUNCTION WITH THESE FACILITIES IS NORMALLY SQUADRON PROPERTY.
description: THESE ARE MARINE CORPS FACILITIES REQUIRED TO ACCOMMODATE, IN- GARRISON, THE EQUIPMENT USED FOR EXPEDITIONARY AIRCRAFT COMMAND AND CONTROL. THESE FACILITIES ARE ASSIGNED TO SPECIALIZED MARINE CORPS SQUADRONS, AND THE EXPEDITIONARY EQUIPMENT USED IN CONJUNCTION WITH THESE FACILITIES IS NORMALLY SQUADRON PROPERTY.

title: OPERATIONS SUPPORT SHED
subCategory: 149 OP FAC OTHER THAN BLDGS This category group contains facilities such as towers and structures which support tactical or organizational aircraft related operations and which do not fall readily into another category. It includes protective construction.
notes: STORAGE SHED OR OTHER COVERED AREA USED FOR OPERATIONAL SUPPORT. NOT FOR WEAPONS CLEANING AREAS.
description: STORAGE SHED OR OTHER COVERED AREA USED FOR OPERATIONAL SUPPORT. NOT FOR WEAPONS CLEANING AREAS.

title: AMMUNITION PIER
subCategory: 151 PIERS Includes all piers regardless of function served, protective dolphins at pier heads, fendering systems, mooring fixtures, original dredging performed specifically for the purpose of providing the pier facility, all trackage on the pier, and all supporting utilities and services.
notes: AMMUNITION PIERS ARE DESIGNED FOR USE IN THE RECEIPT OF AMMUNITION FOR STORAGE AND FOR THE OUTLOADING OF AMMUNITION ONTO BARGES AND SHIPS. IN SOME CASES OUTGOING AMMUNITION IS FIRST LOADED FROM THE AMMUNITION PIER ONTO BARGES FOR TRANSFER TO SHIPS MOORED OFFSHORE OR IN A ROADSTEAD.
description: AMMUNITION PIERS ARE DESIGNED FOR USE IN THE RECEIPT OF AMMUNITION FOR STORAGE AND FOR THE OUTLOADING OF AMMUNITION ONTO BARGES AND SHIPS. IN SOME CASES OUTGOING AMMUNITION IS FIRST LOADED FROM THE AMMUNITION PIER ONTO BARGES FOR TRANSFER TO SHIPS MOORED OFFSHORE OR IN A ROADSTEAD.

title: GENL PURP/BERTHING PIER
subCategory: 151 PIERS Includes all piers regardless of function served, protective dolphins at pier heads, fendering systems, mooring fixtures, original dredging performed specifically for the purpose of providing the pier facility, all trackage on the pier, and all supporting utilities and services.
notes: GENERAL PURPOSE BERTHING PIERS ARE USED PRIMARILY FOR MOORING HOME PORTED AND TRANSIENT SHIPS THAT DO NOT REQUIRE PIERS EQUIPPED WITH SHIPYARD FACILITIES. BERTHING PIERS ARE CLASSIFIED AS ACTIVE OR INACTIVE. THE ACTIVE BERTHING PIERS ARE USED WHEN SHIPS ARE BERTHED FOR A RELATIVELY SHORT TIME; THE INACTIVE CLASSES ARE USED WHEN SHIPS ARE TO BE TIED UP FOR LONG PERIODS IN A DECOMMISSIONED STATUS.
description: GENERAL PURPOSE BERTHING PIERS ARE USED PRIMARILY FOR MOORING HOME PORTED AND TRANSIENT SHIPS THAT DO NOT REQUIRE PIERS EQUIPPED WITH SHIPYARD FACILITIES. BERTHING PIERS ARE CLASSIFIED AS ACTIVE OR INACTIVE. THE ACTIVE BERTHING PIERS ARE USED WHEN SHIPS ARE BERTHED FOR A RELATIVELY SHORT TIME; THE INACTIVE CLASSES ARE USED WHEN SHIPS ARE TO BE TIED UP FOR LONG PERIODS IN A DECOMMISSIONED STATUS.

title: FUELING PIER
subCategory: 151 PIERS Includes all piers regardless of function served, protective dolphins at pier heads, fendering systems, mooring fixtures, original dredging performed specifically for the purpose of providing the pier facility, all trackage on the pier, and all supporting utilities and services.
notes: FACILITIES FOR BERTHING SHIPS WHILE DISCHARGING FUEL TO STORAGE OR RECEIVING FUEL FROM STORAGE ARE PROVIDED AT FUELING PIERS. SUCH PIERS WILL PROVIDE SALT WATER FOR FIREFIGHTING, TELEPHONE AND FIRE ALARM FACILITIES AND MAY PROVIDE FRESHWATER, STEAM IN COLD CLIMATES, ELECTRIC POWER.
description: FACILITIES FOR BERTHING SHIPS WHILE DISCHARGING FUEL TO STORAGE OR RECEIVING FUEL FROM STORAGE ARE PROVIDED AT FUELING PIERS. SUCH PIERS WILL PROVIDE SALT WATER FOR FIREFIGHTING, TELEPHONE AND FIRE ALARM FACILITIES AND MAY PROVIDE FRESHWATER, STEAM IN COLD CLIMATES, ELECTRIC POWER.

title: REPAIR PIER
subCategory: 151 PIERS Includes all piers regardless of function served, protective dolphins at pier heads, fendering systems, mooring fixtures, original dredging performed specifically for the purpose of providing the pier facility, all trackage on the pier, and all supporting utilities and services.
notes: REPAIR PIERS ARE CONSTRUCTED AND EQUIPPED TO PERMIT OVERHAUL OF THOSE PORTIONS OF A VESSEL ABOVE THE WATERLINE. THESE STRUCTURES WILL NORMALLY BE EQUIPPED WITH A GANTRY CRANE AND STANDARD-GAGE RAILROAD TRACKS AND HAVE FACILITIES TO PROVIDE SALT AND FRESHWATER, STEAM, COMPRESSED AIR, TELEPHONE AND FIRE ALARM SERVICE, AND ELECTRIC POWER FOR SHIP SERVICE, LIGHTING AND WELDING.
description: REPAIR PIERS ARE CONSTRUCTED AND EQUIPPED TO PERMIT OVERHAUL OF THOSE PORTIONS OF A VESSEL ABOVE THE WATERLINE. THESE STRUCTURES WILL NORMALLY BE EQUIPPED WITH A GANTRY CRANE AND STANDARD-GAGE RAILROAD TRACKS AND HAVE FACILITIES TO PROVIDE SALT AND FRESHWATER, STEAM, COMPRESSED AIR, TELEPHONE AND FIRE ALARM SERVICE, AND ELECTRIC POWER FOR SHIP SERVICE, LIGHTING AND WELDING.

title: SUPPLY PIER
subCategory: 151 PIERS Includes all piers regardless of function served, protective dolphins at pier heads, fendering systems, mooring fixtures, original dredging performed specifically for the purpose of providing the pier facility, all trackage on the pier, and all supporting utilities and services.
notes: SUPPLY PIERS ACCOMMODATE BERTHING FOR THE TRANSFER OF MATERIALS BETWEEN SHIP AND SHORE. A LARGE BUILDING OR TRANSIT SHED NORMALLY OCCUPIES THE CENTRAL PORTION OF A SUPPLY PIER.
description: SUPPLY PIERS ACCOMMODATE BERTHING FOR THE TRANSFER OF MATERIALS BETWEEN SHIP AND SHORE. A LARGE BUILDING OR TRANSIT SHED NORMALLY OCCUPIES THE CENTRAL PORTION OF A SUPPLY PIER.

title: ORDNANCE CONT HANDL PIER
subCategory: 151 PIERS Includes all piers regardless of function served, protective dolphins at pier heads, fendering systems, mooring fixtures, original dredging performed specifically for the purpose of providing the pier facility, all trackage on the pier, and all supporting utilities and services.
notes: AN ORDNANCE CONTAINER HANDLING PIER IS USED PRIMARILY FOR THE OUTLOADING AND RECEIVING OF EXPLOSIVE ORDNANCE IN CONTAINERS FROM NON-SELF-SUSTAINING CONTAINER SHIPS.
description: AN ORDNANCE CONTAINER HANDLING PIER IS USED PRIMARILY FOR THE OUTLOADING AND RECEIVING OF EXPLOSIVE ORDNANCE IN CONTAINERS FROM NON-SELF-SUSTAINING CONTAINER SHIPS.

title: DEGAUSSING PIER
subCategory: 151 PIERS Includes all piers regardless of function served, protective dolphins at pier heads, fendering systems, mooring fixtures, original dredging performed specifically for the purpose of providing the pier facility, all trackage on the pier, and all supporting utilities and services.
notes: THESE PIERS ARE USED TO ERASE THE MAGNETIC SIGNATURE OF A SHIP OR SUBMARINE. SIZING FOR THIS CATEGORY CODE IS BASED ON THE TYPE OF VESSELS TO BE SERVICED AND IS DRIVEN BY THE NAVSEA SPECIFIED EQUIPMENT. SPECIAL STUDIES ARE REQUIRED ON A CASE BY CASE BASIS.

title: ACCESS TRESTLE PIERS WHRVS
subCategory: 151 PIERS Includes all piers regardless of function served, protective dolphins at pier heads, fendering systems, mooring fixtures, original dredging performed specifically for the purpose of providing the pier facility, all trackage on the pier, and all supporting utilities and services.
notes: A VEHICULAR AND/OR RAILROAD TRESTLE CONNECTING SHORE FACILITIES TO A PIER OR WHARF.
description: A VEHICULAR AND/OR RAILROAD TRESTLE CONNECTING SHORE FACILITIES TO A PIER OR WHARF.

title: AMMUNITION WHARF
subCategory: 152 WHARFS A wharf is an open type marginal structure for the berthing of vessels; it is usually connected to the shore at more than one point. In general, the planning criteria that apply to piers are also applicable to wharves. Either may serve the same practicable purpose, however, since their physical design and layout will be much different, their capacities for berthing and cargo handling will vary. Piers are generally preferable structures; however, certain locations will dictate the use of a wharf rather than a pier because of the marginal fairway and topography involved.
notes: AMMUNITION WHARVES ARE DESIGNED FOR USE IN THE RECEIPT OF AMMUNITION FOR STORAGE AND FOR THE OUTLOADING OF AMMUNITION ONTO BARGES AND SHIPS. IN SOME CASES OUTGOING AMMUNITION IS FIRST LOADED FROM THE AMMUNITION PIER ONTO BARGES FOR TRANSFER TO SHIPS MOORED OFFSHORE OR IN A ROADSTEAD.
description: AMMUNITION WHARVES ARE DESIGNED FOR USE IN THE RECEIPT OF AMMUNITION FOR STORAGE AND FOR THE OUTLOADING OF AMMUNITION ONTO BARGES AND SHIPS. IN SOME CASES OUTGOING AMMUNITION IS FIRST LOADED FROM THE AMMUNITION PIER ONTO BARGES FOR TRANSFER TO SHIPS MOORED OFFSHORE OR IN A ROADSTEAD.

title: FITTING OUT WHARF
subCategory: 152 WHARFS A wharf is an open type marginal structure for the berthing of vessels; it is usually connected to the shore at more than one point. In general, the planning criteria that apply to piers are also applicable to wharves. Either may serve the same practicable purpose, however, since their physical design and layout will be much different, their capacities for berthing and cargo handling will vary. Piers are generally preferable structures; however, certain locations will dictate the use of a wharf rather than a pier because of the marginal fairway and topography involved.
notes: WHARVES FOR FITTING OUT ARE VERY SIMILAR TO THOSE USED FOR REPAIR PURPOSES, PROVIDING APPROXIMATELY THE SAME FACILITIES. HOWEVER, FITTING OUT PIERS WILL HAVE, IN ADDITION TO LIGHT AND HEAVY GANTRY CRANES, A LARGE FIXED TOWER CRANE FOR HANDLING GUNS, TURRETS, ENGINES, AND HEAVY ARMOR.

title: CARGO STAGING AREA
subCategory: 153 CARGO HANDLING FACILITIES A cargo staging area is an open hardstand for temporary storage of cargo awaiting further transshipment.
notes: A CARGO STAGING AREA IS AN OPEN HARDSTAND FOR TEMPORARY STORAGE OF CARGO AWAITING FURTHER TRANSSHIPMENT.
description: A CARGO STAGING AREA IS AN OPEN HARDSTAND FOR TEMPORARY STORAGE OF CARGO AWAITING FURTHER TRANSSHIPMENT.

title: BLKHD QW NO RELIEVG PLATFM
subCategory: 154 SEAWLS/BULKHDS/Q UAYWLS Shore protective structures not intended primarily for berthing vessels. Bulkheads and quaywalls have the principal advantage of affording accessibility for their entire length along the foreshore.
notes: THIS TYPE OF BULKHEAD OR QUAYWALL IS A STRUCTURE TO RETAIN EARTH ALONG A SHORELINE IN SHALLOW WATER. THE DEPTH OF WATER IS TYPICALLY LIMITED TO A 25 FEET AND THE STRUCTURE HAS NO RELIEVING PLATFORM. THIS STRUCTURE DOES NOT PROVIDE SHIP BERTHING (151 PIERS AND 152 WHARVES SHOULD BE USED FOR ANY SHIP BERTHING REQUIREMENT).
description: THIS TYPE OF BULKHEAD OR QUAYWALL IS A STRUCTURE TO RETAIN EARTH ALONG A SHORELINE IN SHALLOW WATER. THIS STRUCTURE DOES NOT PROVIDE SHIP BERTHING (151 PIERS AND 152 WHARVES SHOULD BE USED FOR ANY SHIP BERTHING REQUIREMENT).

title: BLKHD QW WITH R PLATFM
subCategory: 154 SEAWLS/BULKHDS/Q UAYWLS Shore protective structures not intended primarily for berthing vessels. Bulkheads and quaywalls have the principal advantage of affording accessibility for their entire length along the foreshore.
notes: THIS TYPE OF BULKHEAD OR QUAYWALL IS A STRUCTURE TO RETAIN EARTH ALONG A SHORELINE IN DEEP WATER. TYPICALLY WITH A WATER DEPTH EXCEEDING 25 FEET AND INCLUDES A RELIEVING PLATFORM TO SUPPORT HEAVY LOGISTICS OPERATIONS. THIS STRUCTURE DOES NOT PROVIDE SHIP BERTHING (151 PIERS AND 152 WHARVES SHOULD BE USED FOR ANY SHIP BERTHING REQUIREMENT).
description: THIS TYPE OF BULKHEAD OR QUAYWALL IS A STRUCTURE TO RETAIN EARTH ALONG A SHORELINE IN DEEP WATER.
